questions.question: Which platform currently has the highest two-qubit gate fidelity?
questions.explanation: Trapped ions have demonstrated >99.9% two-qubit gate fidelity, the highest among current platforms, though they are slower than superconducting qubits.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of photonic quantum computing for temperature?

options.text: It operates at liquid nitrogen temperature

questions.explanation: Photonic quantum computing operates at room temperature, avoiding expensive cryogenic systems. This makes it attractive for deployment and quantum networks.

questions.question: Which platform naturally provides all-to-all qubit connectivity?
questions.explanation: Trapped ions in a chain share motional modes, allowing any ion to directly interact with any other without SWAP gates.

questions.question: Which platform has the fastest gate operations?
questions.explanation: Superconducting qubits have the fastest gates at ~20-50 ns, about 1000× faster than trapped ions, though with lower fidelity.

questions.question: Which platform is best suited for quantum networking applications?
questions.explanation: Photonic systems excel at quantum networking because photons naturally transmit quantum information, operate at room temperature, and are compatible with telecom infrastructure.

questions.question: What unique capability do neutral atom arrays have?
questions.explanation: Neutral atoms in optical tweezers can be physically rearranged during computation, allowing dynamic reconfiguration of the connectivity graph.

questions.question: There is a single quantum computing platform that dominates all performance metrics.
questions.explanation: False. Each platform makes different trade-offs. Superconducting is fastest but less coherent, ions are highest quality but slower, atoms are scalable, photons work at room temp. No single winner exists.
